q: According to Newton's second law, an object that is accelerating must have a ___ acting on it.
This force is known as the ___.
In the absence of this force, the object would not move along a circle but move in a ___, according to Newton's first law.
The ___ in the string provides the required centripetal force for the ball to keep moving in circular path.
An attractive force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction ___ to the path of the earth.
The Coulomb attractive force between the nucleus and the electron acts as the required centripetal force for the electron to move around the ___.
When a car turns in a circular path or a curve, a ___ exists between the road and the car tires.
The ___ of normal force is the required centripetal force.
On a flat road (unbanked road), this force is supplied by ___ between the tires and the road.
Banking of roads is raising the ___ of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The angle that the inclined track makes with horizontal is called angle of ___.
The normal force $F_{N}$ exerted by a banked road is ___ to the road.
In this case, the horizontal component of the normal force, $F_{N} \sin \theta$, towards the centre of the curve of radius $r$ just equals the centripetal force (___).
